libraryDescription:
The VCSSL Graphics3D library is one of the standard libraries for handling graphics in VCSSL,and provides animation-capable 3D computer graphics (3DCG) rendering functions.

Because the Graphics3D library is relatively large, it is difficult to use it effectively by referring only to the function specifications described here.For specific usage examples and guidance, refer to the "Official VCSSL 3DCG Development Guide."

variable0.name: QUADRANGLE_LIST
variable0.description: The coordinate-array format that directly stores the coordinate values of a quadrangle-polygon set. It is used by functions such as newModel.

variable1.name: QUADRANGLE_GRID
variable1.description: The coordinate-array format that stores the coordinates of grid points in a surface-like arrangement of quadrangle polygons. It is used by functions such as newModel.

variable2.name: TRIANGLE_LIST
variable2.description: The coordinate-array format that directly stores the coordinate values of a triangle-polygon set. It is used by functions such as newModel.

variable3.name: TRIANGLE_FAN
variable3.description: The coordinate-array format that stores the coordinates of the center and arc in a fan-shaped arrangement of triangle polygons. It is used by functions such as newModel.

variable4.name: TRIANGLE_STRIP
variable4.description: The coordinate-array format in which adjacent triangle polygons arranged in sequence share vertices. It is used by functions such as newModel.

variable5.name: VIEW
variable5.description: The constant representing the view coordinate system. It is used by functions such as setPolygonVertexOffset and setModelVertexOffset.

variable6.name: LOCAL
variable6.description: The constant representing the local coordinate system. It is used by functions such as setPolygonVertexOffset and setModelVertexOffset.

function3.name: clearGraphics3D
function3.declaration: macro clearGraphics3D( int rendererID, bool removeModel, bool removeLight, bool removeCoordinate )
function3.description: Clears the drawing contents with the background color and, if necessary, removes all solids (models and polygons), lights, and coordinate systems.
function3.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(bool type) removeModel : Whether to remove all solids. 
(bool type) removeLight : Whether to remove all lights. 
(bool type) removeCoordinate : Whether to remove all coordinate systems.

function4.name: setGraphics3DMagnification
function4.declaration: void setGraphics3DMagnification( int rendererID, float magnification )
function4.description: Sets the rendering magnification. A line segment of length 1.0 located at a distance of 1.0 from the viewpoint is rendered with a length of magnification pixels.By combining this with the setGraphics3DDistance function, the strength of perspective can also be adjusted. Displaying nearby objects smaller emphasizes perspective, while displaying distant objects larger weakens it.
function4.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(float type) magnification : The rendering magnification.

function5.declaration: void setGraphics3DAngle( int rendererID, float angle )
function5.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(float type) angle : The angle of view.

function6.name: setGraphics3DDistance
function6.declaration: void setGraphics3DDistance( int rendererID, float distance )
function6.description: Sets the distance between the screen and the point being looked at. When this function is called, the world-coordinate origin is automatically adjusted so that it is always located on the Z axis of the view coordinate system. Then the distance between the origins of the world and view coordinate systems becomes the value specified by the argument distance.By combining this with the setGraphics3DMagnification function, the strength of perspective can also be adjusted. Displaying nearby objects smaller emphasizes perspective, while displaying distant objects larger weakens it.
function6.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(float type) distance : The camera distance.

function7.name: setGraphics3DCenter
function7.declaration: void setGraphics3DCenter( int rendererID, int x, int y )
function7.description: Sets the drawing-center coordinates. The drawing center here is the position on the screen that coincides with the origin of the view coordinate system, and visually corresponds to the so-called vanishing point in one-point perspective.For example, even with the same camera angle, setting the screen center lower (a smaller Y coordinate) shows more of the sky. Conversely, setting it higher (a larger Y coordinate) shows more of the ground.
function7.parameters:
(int type) rendererID : The renderer ID. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the center, where the bottom-left corner of the drawing area is 0. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the center, where the bottom-left corner of the drawing area is 0.
